description: Golden graph and query truth snapshot for the B-7 end-to-end corpus gate. B-7 diffs a live run against this file. Update this file under review when intentional graph behaviour changes; never update silently. `corpus_id` is a stable identifier (its historical `-20repo` suffix is not a live count); `corpus_composition.git_repos` is the authoritative repository count.
tolerance_policy: count ranges are [min, max] inclusive; a live run outside the range fails B-7(b). required_correlations assertions must all pass; a missing edge fails B-7(b). query_shapes define required response fields; a missing field fails B-7(c).

Repository.note: 24 corpus repos; may include additional discovered mirror stubs

File.note: all source files parsed across the deterministic corpus (observed 78); floor catches a major projection drop (e.g. #4019), ceiling stays wide for parser growth

Function.note: all parsed function definitions across the corpus

WorkloadInstance.note: logical workload-instance nodes derived from config materialization and deployment mapping. Floor raised 1->4 by #5444 (broadened environment detection beyond path regexes, full-corpus-mode-only per doc.go -- required_nodes is existence-mode-blocking with no full/existence split, so a corpus-scale floor belongs here, not there): before #5444 only workload-instance:deployable-config:prod and workload-instance:kubernetes_comprehensive:prod existed (count=2, empirically verified on origin/main e15b3089c with this exact 25-repo corpus). #5444 reads kustomize-deployable-overlay/kustomization.yaml's namespace: production field (kustomize_overlays[].namespace, alias-canonicalized to prod by namespaceEnvironment in go/internal/reducer/environment_signals.go) into deploymentEnvironments["kustomize-deployable-overlay"] -- read directly by kustomize-deployable-overlay's own candidate and, via deployable-source's resolved DeploymentRepoIDs=[kustomize-deployable-overlay], by deployable-source's candidate too -- adding workload-instance:deployable-source:prod and workload-instance:kustomize-deployable-overlay:prod (count=4, empirically verified on branch 5444-broaden-env-detection). Neither new instance's own repo files match ExtractOverlayEnvironments's four directory-pattern regexes, and deployable-source carries no k8s_resources of its own (so namespaceEnvironmentFallback is also empty), so this floor fails at 2 if the broadened detection is reverted. See rc-20 for the companion DEPLOYMENT_SOURCE edge-count proof.

KubernetesWorkload.note: raw k8s Deployment/ReplicaSet/StatefulSet/DaemonSet/Pod workload nodes; source of RUNS_IMAGE edges; +1 pod from #5432; #5431 adds observed-vs-desired runtime-status fields (desired/ready/available replicas, pod phase) to the underlying pod_template facts, fact-level only, no node/edge count change; #5433 adds StatefulSet and DaemonSet nodes (+2, min 3->5) plus Job/CronJob collection (no new cassette node for those two kinds), using images that do not correlate to the OciImageManifest cassette so RUNS_IMAGE stays unchanged; #5437 adds a second Pod node (supply-chain-demo-pod-2, +1) plus two kubernetes_live.relationship selector_match facts (a synthetic Service's selector matching both supply-chain-demo-pod and supply-chain-demo-pod-2, proving the one-selector-to-many-pods case), using an image that does not correlate to the OciImageManifest cassette so RUNS_IMAGE stays unchanged; selector_match is provenance-only correlation evidence like owner_reference and ingress_to_service -- it never becomes a Cypher edge, so it surfaces only through the existing list_kubernetes_correlations/GET /api/v0/kubernetes/correlations query shapes, not a new required_correlations or edge_counts entry

KubernetesNamespace.note: raw k8s Namespace nodes materialized from kubernetes_live.namespace facts (issue #5434); the cassette carries exactly 2 -- payments-prod (labeled environment=prod, binds an Environment via TARGETS_ENVIRONMENT, see rc-158) and default (labeled environment=misc-team, a recognized label KEY with an unrecognized VALUE, so it stays environment-unbound with no Environment edge -- the IsKnownToken negative control)

required_correlations.description: At least one Repository(source) CORRELATES_DEPLOYABLE_UNIT Repository(deployment-config); confirms the deployable-unit correlation materializer ran
At least one Function RUNS_IN a Workload; confirms code-to-workload binding ran (RUNS_IN goes Function→Workload, not WorkloadInstance→CloudResource)
Cross-repo Repository DEPENDS_ON Repository edges exist; confirms the B-13 fix — repo_dependency shared_projection_intents drained correctly
At least one KubernetesWorkload RUNS_IMAGE an OciImageManifest; confirms the supply-chain-demo OCI image is correlated to the k8s workload
At least one AtlantisProject MANAGES a Directory; confirms the atlantis.yaml project-to-Terraform-dir governance edge formed for the terraform_comprehensive fixture
At least one AtlantisProject ATLANTIS_DEPENDS_ON another AtlantisProject; confirms the atlantis.yaml depends_on ordering edge formed
At least one AtlantisProject USES_WORKFLOW an AtlantisWorkflow; confirms the atlantis.yaml project->custom-workflow edge formed
At least one Function HANDLES_ROUTE an Endpoint; confirms the api-svc Flask route handlers bound to their Endpoint nodes (same fixture as rc-2 RUNS_IN; code-path edge, no new cassette)
A Function CALLS another Function; the code call graph projected from the comprehensive code fixtures.
A Class INHERITS another Class; class hierarchy projected from the code fixtures.
A Function REFERENCES a Struct; symbol reference projected from the Go fixtures.
A Repository DEFINES a Workload; workload definition projected from in-repo deploy manifests.
A Function INSTANTIATES a Class; construction edge projected from the code fixtures.
A WorkloadInstance is an INSTANCE_OF a Workload; runtime instance correlation (workload materialization, same machinery as rc-2).
A Workload EXPOSES_ENDPOINT an Endpoint; runtime endpoint exposure (workload materialization, same machinery as rc-2/rc-8).
A WorkloadInstance RUNS_ON a Platform; runtime placement correlation (workload materialization).
A Repository DEPLOYS_FROM another Repository; deployment-source correlation (same deployable machinery as rc-1).

#5441 P1 finding 3 regression guard: the helm_argocd_platform fixture's application.yaml declares source.targetRevision: v1.4.0 pointing at the in-corpus deployable-source repository (github.com/acme/deployable-source.git), which the relationships engine emits as ARGOCD_APPLICATION_SOURCE evidence resolving a DEPLOYS_FROM edge. DEPLOYS_FROM is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by Kustomize (rc-29) and a Helm chart dependency (rc-34), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[ARGOCD_APPLICATION_SOURCE] to isolate the ArgoCD deployment-source verb, and additionally requires the edge to carry a non-empty source_revision property -- the exact regression this issue fixed: buildResolvedEdgeIntentRow previously read the wrong Details map and always wrote source_revision as "" for every ArgoCD-sourced edge in production. The count and the property are both provably zero/empty without the fix.

#5434 namespace environment-alias binding, closed bound (both a floor and a ceiling, mirroring sl-dart-calls-recursion's pattern): the cassette carries exactly 2 kubernetes_live.namespace facts -- payments-prod, labeled environment=prod (a recognized token), and default, labeled environment=misc-team (a RECOGNIZED label key with an UNRECOGNIZED value -- not IsKnownToken -- so it stays environment-unbound). minimum_count=1 is non-vacuous: it is provably zero without the payments-prod fact's binding logic running (KubernetesNamespaceMaterializationHandler / namespaceEnvironmentFromLabels), proving the POSITIVE case -- an alias-recognized label binds a TARGETS_ENVIRONMENT edge to a canonical Environment node. maximum_count=1 is the NEGATIVE regression anchor and now genuinely exercises the environment.IsKnownToken guard (codex review finding P2, #5434): the prior default fixture carried NO environment-ish label key at all, so it stayed unbound regardless of the guard and the ceiling passed vacuously even with IsKnownToken removed. With a recognized key (environment) but an unrecognized value (misc-team), removing IsKnownToken from namespaceEnvironmentFromLabels would canonicalize and bind default too, pushing this count to 2 and failing the ceiling; the guard in place keeps it unbound and the count at 1. from_label/relationship/to_label alone isolate the producer: no other domain writes a TARGETS_ENVIRONMENT edge FROM a KubernetesNamespace node.

#5441 P1 finding 3 regression guard: USES_MODULE had zero golden-corpus coverage before this fix. The terraform_comprehensive fixture's modules.tf eks module previously declared source = git::https://github.com/example/terraform-aws-eks.git?ref=v1.0.0, an org not in the 20-repo catalog, so it never resolved to a real edge -- exactly the same class of gap that let source_revision (rc-156) sit uncovered before the live gate caught it. Repointed at the in-corpus deployable-source repository (source = git::https://github.com/acme/deployable-source.git?ref=v1.0.0) so the module-source pin is observable end to end: the relationships engine emits TERRAFORM_MODULE_SOURCE evidence resolving a USES_MODULE edge, and evidenceFactFirstPartyRefVersion (evidence_edge_fields.go) derives first_party_ref_version from Details["source_ref"] via ExtractTerraformRefPin since Terraform module evidence never sets first_party_ref_version directly (unlike GitHub Actions). The count and the property are both provably zero/empty without the fixture retarget.

A WorkloadInstance DEPLOYMENT_SOURCE a Repository; runtime-to-source correlation (workload materialization). Floor raised 1->3 by #5444 (broadened environment detection beyond path regexes): before #5444 only workload-instance:deployable-config:prod produced this edge (count=1, empirically verified on origin/main e15b3089c with this exact 25-repo corpus). #5444 reads kustomize-deployable-overlay's kustomization.yaml namespace:production (kustomize_overlays[].namespace, alias-canonicalized to prod) into deploymentEnvironments, which the deployable-source candidate's DeploymentRepoIDs=[kustomize-deployable-overlay] resolves into a NEW workload-instance:deployable-source:prod carrying two more DEPLOYMENT_SOURCE edges (to kustomize-deployable-overlay and to its own deployable-config-app dependency), for a total of 3 (empirically verified on branch 5444-broaden-env-detection). This floor fails at 1 if the broadened detection is reverted -- see graph.node_counts.WorkloadInstance for the companion WorkloadInstance node-count proof (also full-mode-only).
A Repository HAS_DEPLOYMENT_EVIDENCE an EvidenceArtifact; deployment evidence correlation.
An EvidenceArtifact EVIDENCES_REPOSITORY_RELATIONSHIP a Repository; evidence provenance edge.
A Class USES_METACLASS another Class; Python metaclass edge projected from the python fixtures.

A Function INVOKES_CLOUD_ACTION a CloudAction; the go_comprehensive cloud_actions.go AWS-SDK call site (s3.PutObject) resolves to the s3:putobject CAN_PERFORM catalog action. Regression guard for the filterUpsertRows action-field collision that previously dropped every such upsert.

A PackageDependency DEPENDS_ON_PACKAGE a Package; the package_registry supply-chain-demo cassette carries a package_version + package_dependency fact for github.com/acme/lib-common@1.0.0 declaring a direct dependency on github.com/acme/synthetic-dep, and the package_registry canonical writer materialises the dependency edge. Requires the non-git source-local projector path to run during the gate.

A Package HAS_VERSION a PackageVersion; the package_registry supply-chain-demo cassette's package + package_version facts project the version node and the HAS_VERSION edge via the deferred package_registry edge group (rc-9 NornicDB fix). Locks in the package_registry canonical version edge.

A PackageVersion DECLARES_DEPENDENCY a PackageDependency; the package_registry package_version + package_dependency facts project the dependency node and the DECLARES_DEPENDENCY edge via the deferred package_registry edge group. The version-side companion to rc-9's DEPENDS_ON_PACKAGE.

A Repository PROVISIONS_PLATFORM a Platform; the terragrunt_comprehensive fixture's HCL declares cluster/platform resources (EKS/VPC/RDS module families) that the reducer infrastructure-platform extractor recognises, and the InfrastructurePlatformMaterializer projects the Platform node plus the PROVISIONS_PLATFORM edge. The base terraform_comprehensive fixture alone does not emit it (verified count=0 without terragrunt), so this locks in the terragrunt/IaC platform-provisioning verb.

A GitlabPipeline DEFINES_JOB a GitlabJob; the terraform_comprehensive fixture carries a depth-0 .gitlab-ci.yml whose validate/plan jobs are declared by the single pipeline node. Static-parse edge, no cassette.

A GitlabJob NEEDS another GitlabJob; the terraform_comprehensive .gitlab-ci.yml terraform-plan job declares needs: [terraform-validate], resolved to the sibling job within the same file. Distinct NEEDS edge type, not the generic DEPENDS_ON.

A Repository DEPLOYS_FROM another Repository via Kustomize; the kustomize-deployable-overlay fixture's kustomization.yaml declares a remote Kustomize base github.com/acme/deployable-source//k8s, which the relationships engine emits as KUSTOMIZE_RESOURCE_REFERENCE evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DEPLOYS_FROM is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by ArgoCD (rc-19), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[KUSTOMIZE_RESOURCE_REFERENCE] to isolate the Kustomize deployment-source verb; the count is provably zero without the kustomize-deployable-overlay fixture.

A Repository DEPENDS_ON another Repository via an Ansible role reference; the ansible-platform-playbooks fixture's playbooks/site.yml applies the ansible-shared-roles role, which the relationships engine emits as ANSIBLE_ROLE_REFERENCE evidence resolving to the in-corpus ansible-shared-roles repository. DEPENDS_ON is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by package consumption (rc-3), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[ANSIBLE_ROLE_REFERENCE] to isolate the Ansible role-dependency verb; the count is provably zero without the ansible-platform-playbooks fixture.

A Repository DISCOVERS_CONFIG_IN another Repository via a Jenkins pipeline; the jenkins-ci-pipelines fixture's Jenkinsfile checks out github.com/acme/deployable-source, which the relationships engine emits as JENKINS_GITHUB_REPOSITORY evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DISCOVERS_CONFIG_IN is also emitted by Terraform, Terragrunt and GitHub Actions, so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[JENKINS_GITHUB_REPOSITORY] to isolate the Jenkins config-discovery verb; the count is provably zero without the jenkins-ci-pipelines fixture.

A Repository DEPENDS_ON another Repository via a Puppet module reference; the puppet-platform-modules fixture's Puppetfile declares an r10k mod acme-base whose :git source is github.com/acme/deployable-source, which the relationships engine emits as PUPPET_MODULE_REFERENCE evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DEPENDS_ON is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by package consumption (rc-3) and Ansible roles (rc-30), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[PUPPET_MODULE_REFERENCE] to isolate the Puppet module-dependency verb; the count is provably zero without the puppet-platform-modules fixture.

A Repository DEPENDS_ON another Repository via a Chef cookbook dependency; the chef-cookbooks fixture's Berksfile declares a Berkshelf cookbook acme-base whose git: source is github.com/acme/deployable-source, which the relationships engine emits as CHEF_COOKBOOK_DEPENDENCY evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DEPENDS_ON is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by package consumption (rc-3), Ansible roles (rc-30) and Puppet modules (rc-32), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[CHEF_COOKBOOK_DEPENDENCY] to isolate the Chef cookbook-dependency verb; the count is provably zero without the chef-cookbooks fixture.

A Repository DEPLOYS_FROM another Repository via a Helm chart dependency; the helm-umbrella-chart fixture's Chart.yaml declares a subchart dependency whose repository is github.com/acme/deployable-source, which the relationships engine emits as HELM_CHART_REFERENCE evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DEPLOYS_FROM is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by ArgoCD (rc-19) and Kustomize (rc-29), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[HELM_CHART_REFERENCE] to isolate the Helm chart-dependency deployment-source verb; the count is provably zero without the helm-umbrella-chart fixture.

A Helm chart template references a values.yaml definition via .Values; the helm-template-chart fixture uses {{ .Values.<dotted.path> }} expressions that the parser resolves to the matching leaf keys in the same chart's values.yaml. templates/deployment.yaml contributes 7 usages (replicaCount, image.repository, image.tag, image.pullPolicy, service.port, resources.limits.cpu, resources.limits.memory) and the NESTED templates/config/configmap.yaml contributes service.type (8 total). The nested manifest is the regression guard for #4019: NornicDB previously dropped every file (and its entities) nested >=2 directories deep because the canonical directory write MATCH'd a parent directory MERGE'd in the same transaction; the minimum_count of 8 fails if that depth-2 projection regresses. Each usage becomes a HelmTemplateValueUsage content entity, each leaf a HelmValueDefinition content entity, and the projector structural-edge phase links usage to definition with a dedicated HELM_VALUE_REFERENCE edge. This edge previously reused the shared REFERENCES type (also emitted for code-symbol references, rc-13), but a full-refresh retract of that shared type is O(all-REFERENCES) per delete and blew the canonical-write budget at repo scale (#4464, #4476); a dedicated type keeps the retract's delete-index small. The edge still carries evidence_kinds=[HELM_TEMPLATE_VALUE_REFERENCE], so this correlation asserts both the dedicated relationship type and that evidence kind; the count is provably zero without the helm-template-chart fixture (no other corpus repo defines the HelmTemplateValueUsage / HelmValueDefinition node labels).

A Repository DEPENDS_ON another Repository via a Salt gitfs formula reference; the salt-formulas fixture's master.yaml declares a Salt gitfs_remotes list whose first remote is github.com/acme/deployable-source, which the relationships engine emits as SALT_FORMULA_REFERENCE evidence resolving to the in-corpus deployable-source repository. DEPENDS_ON is a tool-agnostic edge also emitted by package consumption (rc-3), Ansible roles (rc-30), Puppet modules (rc-32) and Chef cookbooks (rc-33), so this correlation filters on evidence_kinds=[SALT_FORMULA_REFERENCE] to isolate the Salt gitfs formula-dependency verb; the count is provably zero without the salt-formulas fixture.

A BigQuery Table CloudResource is connected to its parent Dataset CloudResource via a GCP_bigquery_table_in_dataset edge; the gcpcloud cassette carries a gcp_cloud_resource fact for the events Table (asset_type bigquery.googleapis.com/Table) and one for the analytics Dataset (asset_type bigquery.googleapis.com/Dataset), plus a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type bigquery_table_in_dataset. The GCP relationship materializer resolves both endpoints by full_resource_name and writes the typed edge; GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_, so the materialized edge type is GCP_bigquery_table_in_dataset. The count is provably zero without the GCP cloud_resource + relationship facts in the cassette, so this locks in GCP node projection and the relationship edge path end-to-end.

A Persistent Disk CloudResource is connected to its attached Instance CloudResource via a GCP_disk_attached_to_instance edge; the gcpcloud cassette carries a gcp_cloud_resource fact for the demo-disk Disk (asset_type compute.googleapis.com/Disk) and one for the demo-instance Instance (asset_type compute.googleapis.com/Instance), plus a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type disk_attached_to_instance, matching the #4294 extractor contract. GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_. The count is provably zero without the Disk cloud_resource + relationship facts, so this locks in the Persistent Disk typed-depth extractor's golden-corpus follow-up (#4388).

A Persistent Disk CloudResource is connected to its source Image CloudResource via a GCP_disk_created_from_image edge; the gcpcloud cassette's demo-disk Disk fact carries a correlation anchor to the demo-image Image (asset_type compute.googleapis.com/Image) and a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type disk_created_from_image, matching the #4294 extractor's provenance contract. GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_. The count is provably zero without the Disk-to-Image relationship fact, so this locks in the disk provenance edge path (#4388).

A Persistent Disk CloudResource is connected to its source Snapshot CloudResource via a GCP_disk_created_from_snapshot edge; the gcpcloud cassette's demo-disk Disk fact carries a correlation anchor to the demo-snapshot Snapshot (asset_type compute.googleapis.com/Snapshot) and a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type disk_created_from_snapshot, matching the #4294 extractor's provenance contract. GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_. The count is provably zero without the Disk-to-Snapshot relationship fact, so this locks in the disk provenance edge path (#4388).

A Persistent Disk CloudResource is connected to its Cloud KMS CryptoKey CloudResource via a GCP_disk_encrypted_by_key edge; the gcpcloud cassette carries a gcp_cloud_resource fact for the demo-disk-key CryptoKey (asset_type cloudkms.googleapis.com/CryptoKey) and a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type disk_encrypted_by_key, matching the #4294 extractor's CMEK contract. GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_. The count is provably zero without the Disk CMEK relationship fact, so this locks in the disk encryption edge path (#4388).

A Compute Engine Instance CloudResource is connected to an attached Disk CloudResource via a GCP_instance_uses_disk edge; the gcpcloud cassette's demo-instance Instance fact carries a gcp_cloud_relationship fact with relationship_type instance_uses_disk targeting the demo-disk Disk, matching the #4281 extractor contract. GCPCloudResourceEdgeWriter prefixes the Cypher relationship type with GCP_. required_self_loops.description: Exactly 2 Function{language=dart} CALLS self-loop edges (caller==callee): recursionFib (arrow-form) and recursionFact (block-form) in tests/fixtures/ecosystems/dart_comprehensive/calls.dart. Dart's call extractor (go/internal/parser/dart/calls.go) was a raw byte-scanner that could not distinguish a declaration header from a call site, recording every function/method/constructor declaration in the corpus as a spurious self-loop CALLS edge (issue #5332). Dart was not staged in the golden corpus, so that corruption class shipped with no durable end-to-end gate (issue #5349). The bound is closed on BOTH sides, not a floor: below 2 means genuine recursion was dropped; above 2 means the #5332 declaration-vs-call-site bug regressed (every one of the fixture's other declarations — DeclarationOnlyWidget's 2 constructors + getter + setter + method, GeoPoint's named constructor, mutualPing/mutualPong, identityFn, LineBuffer.append — would each contribute a spurious self-loop). Mutual recursion (mutualPing<->mutualPong) and the named-constructor/generic-args/cascade call shapes in the same fixture are NOT self-loops (caller != callee) and so do not move this count; they instead flow through the existing generic rc-11 Function-CALLS-Function floor.
required_self_loops.label: Function

POST /api/v0/infra/relationships.description: #5436: RUNS_IMAGE read-path proof. Anchored on the supply-chain-demo KubernetesWorkload (the digest-pinned Deployment from the kuberneteslive cassette), what_runs_image must resolve the RUNS_IMAGE edge to the OciImageManifest node the oci_registry cassette's image_manifest fact projects (digest sha256:abcdef...ab). Non-vacuous: the edge only exists because the kuberneteslive pod_template fact's image_refs digest matches the ociregistry image_manifest fact's digest via BuildSourceImageDigestJoinIndex (kubernetes_workload_source_image_join.go) -- neither cassette alone produces it, and before the #5436 Stage-1 allowlist entry this request was rejected 400 (unknown relationship_type).

GET /api/v0/cloud/inventory.description: Cloud resource inventory readback (AWS/Azure/GCP cassettes); confirms cloud facts project to the inventory read surface. required_json_values pins the custom IAM Role's stage attribute (#4401): the gcpcloud cassette's demoCustomRole gcp_cloud_resource fact carries attributes.stage=GA, which is not a Cypher graph-node property (the GCP CloudResource writer's SET clause is a fixed identity/provenance allowlist and never promotes payload.attributes onto the node) but IS surfaced on this Postgres-backed admission readback: cloudInventorySourceFactMappings marks GCPCloudResourceFactKind surfacesAttributes=true, and cloudInventoryResourceView/cloudInventoryAttributes project payload.attributes onto each response resource's attributes map. The assertion is provably zero without the IAM Role cassette fact, so it locks in the Custom IAM Role golden-corpus follow-up on the read surface where its attributes actually project.

POST /api/v0/code/dead-code/investigate?.description: #5378 Detector 1 (Ruby Rails route-handler) golden discrimination. WidgetsController#index is rooted, so it is suppressed as a modeled root; the LegacyReportsController#generate foil is downgraded (not a route), so it must surface as a cleanup_ready dead-code candidate. The two object-matches are closed on (name, classification): over-rooting the foil moves generate out of cleanup_ready and fails the gate (false-positive guard); under-rooting the real handler moves index out of suppressed and fails the gate (false-negative guard). The golden_scope query arg only disambiguates this snapshot key; the handler reads repo_id from the body and ignores the query string. Content-store-only detector: no reducer/graph edge, so no Ifa materialized-edge row applies.

#5378 Detector 2 (Swift Vapor route-handler) golden discrimination. routes.swift has import Vapor, so its use: healthCheck handler is rooted (swift.vapor_route_handler) and suppressed as a modeled root; plain.swift carries the identical use: shape without import Vapor, so its statusReport handler must NOT be rooted and must surface as a cleanup_ready dead-code candidate. The object-matches are closed on (name, classification): rooting the foil (dropping the per-file import Vapor gate) moves statusReport out of cleanup_ready and fails (false-positive guard); losing the healthCheck root moves it out of suppressed and fails (false-negative guard). golden_scope disambiguates the key only; repo_id comes from the body. Content-store-only detector: no reducer/graph edge, so no Ifa materialized-edge row applies.

#5378 Detector 3 (Jenkins Groovy pipeline entrypoint) golden discrimination. The Jenkinsfile pipeline entrypoint is rooted (groovy.jenkins_pipeline_entrypoint) and suppressed as a modeled root; the src/DeployHelper.groovy foil is an ordinary Groovy source file (not a Jenkins artifact), so its deployApp method, though it calls pipelineDeploy(, gets no dead_code_root_kind and must surface as an ambiguous (derived_candidate_only) dead-code candidate. The object-matches are closed on (name, classification): rooting the foil moves deployApp out of ambiguous and fails (false-positive guard); losing the Jenkinsfile root moves it out of suppressed and fails (false-negative guard). golden_scope disambiguates the key only; repo_id comes from the body. Content-store-only detector: no reducer/graph edge, so no Ifa materialized-edge row applies.

POST /api/v0/terraform/config-state-drif.description: HTTP route coverage shape for POST /api/v0/terraform/config-state-drift/findings; asserted independently of the list_terraform_config_state_drift_findings MCP tool shape even though MCP dispatch calls this same route internally, per review: an MCP pass does not prove the HTTP surface was exercised. scope_id is state_snapshot:s3:<sha256(s3://bucket/key)> (terraformstate.ScopeLocatorHash) for bucket=supply-chain-demo-tfstate key=supply-chain-demo/terraform.tfstate, the backend shared by testdata/cassettes/terraformstate/supply-chain-demo.json (state) and tests/fixtures/ecosystems/terraform_comprehensive/main.tf (config), deliberately aligned in issue #5442 so tfstatebackend.ResolveConfigCommitForBackend resolves an owner and real added_in_config/added_in_state drift findings materialize. minimum_results is 1 -- proven by a live golden-corpus-gate run against the HTTP route directly (this entry's own envelope field is false, so the gate sends no Accept: envelope header for this request -- see QueryShape.Envelope's doc comment in go/internal/goldengate/snapshot.go for why that is a per-shape choice, not a property of MCP dispatch), not shape-only.

update_protocol.when: Update this snapshot when an intentional change to graph shape, node labels, relationship types, or canonical query responses lands. Do not update in response to transient corpus drift.
update_protocol.how: Run B-7 locally against the corpus, capture the actual counts and shapes, update the ranges, commit with a clear summary of what changed and why. The PR description must include before/after diffs of this file.
update_protocol.who: Snapshot updates require the same review bar as code changes. Silent updates that widen all ranges without explanation are not acceptable.
